issue.body:
### Goal
Reject a malformed `--max-spend` in `scripts/eval.ts` and `scripts/spike.ts` so a typo can't silently disable the paid-provider spend cap.

### Context / background
Surfaced by sqw-reviewer on PR #51. Both scripts parse the cap as `Number(arg("max-spend") ?? <default>)` with no validation (`scripts/eval.ts:379`, `scripts/spike.ts:104`). A malformed value (e.g. `--max-spend $0.5`) becomes `NaN`; since `spent > NaN` is always `false`, `guard.tripped()` never fires and the circuit breaker is silently defeated — directly against AGENTS.md §4 (money is guarded, non-negotiable).

PR #51 already hardened the same bug class for `scripts/judge.ts` (`parseMaxSpend()` rejects NaN/negative; `parseRepeats()` rejects non-integer). This issue applies the identical guard to the other two call sites.

### Acceptance criteria
- `scripts/eval.ts` and `scripts/spike.ts` reject a non-finite or negative `--max-spend` with a clear error (mirror `parseMaxSpend()` in `scripts/judge.ts`).
- Consider extracting the shared validator into `scripts/lib/spend-guard.ts` (single source) rather than three copies.
- Default behavior (no flag) unchanged.

### Evidence / checks
`bun run verify:pr`. Optionally a unit test on the extracted validator (finite/negative/default).

### Non-goals
No change to the guard's accounting or to `judge.ts` (already done). Not a re-measure.

### Expected artifact
A small PR touching `scripts/eval.ts`, `scripts/spike.ts`, and possibly `scripts/lib/spend-guard.ts` (+ a test if extracted).

### Stop condition
None expected — mechanical hardening. Paid runs are not required to validate it.
issue.title: Harden --max-spend parsing in eval.ts + spike.ts (silent-NaN defeats the spend cap)

pr.body:
## What & why
Closes #52 (filed from a sqw-reviewer finding on #51). `eval.ts:379` and `spike.ts:104` parsed the spend cap as `Number(arg("max-spend") ?? 0.5)` with **no validation**. A malformed value (e.g. `--max-spend \$0.5`) → `NaN`; `spent > NaN` is always false → the circuit breaker **silently never fires** (AGENTS.md §4, money is guarded — non-negotiable).

## Change
- New shared `parseMaxSpend(raw, fallback)` in `scripts/lib/spend-guard.ts` — rejects non-finite/negative with a clear error.
- Used in `eval.ts`, `spike.ts`, and `judge.ts` (dedup — judge.ts had its own copy from #49a). One source for all three spend-guarded scripts.
- Unit-tested (absent→fallback, valid incl. 0, rejects `$0.5`/`abc`/`-1`).

## Checks
`bun run verify:pr` — typecheck ✓, 111 tests ✓ (3 new), Biome ✓.

## Scope
Mechanical hardening of the money guard; no behavior change to a valid invocation. No trust-boundary surface; no paid run needed to validate.

## What I need from the maintainer
No decision beyond merge/block.
pr.title: fix(eval): shared parseMaxSpend rejects a malformed --max-spend cap (#52)
